pr.title: Fix incorrect static in ittomato and mark as matching
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Corrected two ittomato function declarations that had been incorrectly marked static in the C file by moving their prototypes to ittomato.h, then promoted src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The evidence indicates the matching issue was linkage/header visibility rather than a code-generation rewrite.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/ittomato.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/ittomato.c").
Removed local static forward declarations for it_802841B4 and it_8028428C from src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c.
Added non-static prototypes for it_802841B4(Item_GObj* gobj, Vec3* arg1, s32 arg2) and it_8028428C(Item_GObj* gobj) to src/melee/it/items/ittomato.h.
Left other ittomato static/internal declarations in ittomato.c unchanged, including it_80284358 and itTomato_UnkMotion0_* helpers.
smart_moves: Fixed the symbol linkage at the declaration level instead of perturbing function bodies, keeping the matching diff minimal.
Promoted only the functions that needed external visibility to the header while retaining unrelated private helpers as static in the C file.
Updated the build matching status in the same PR as the linkage fix, making the matching transition auditable.
decomp_lessons: If an item file is nearly matching but still marked NonMatching, verify whether functions are incorrectly declared static before attempting instruction-level rewrites.
Functions referenced outside their translation unit should have prototypes in the item header and should not be forward-declared static in the C file.
Minimal linkage corrections can be enough to move a file from NonMatching to Matching when the generated code is otherwise correct.
Keep internal helper routines static, but expose item lifecycle or shared helper functions through the corresponding item header when needed.
naming_conventions: Unidentified address-based item functions retained the it_8028xxxx naming pattern: it_802841B4 and it_8028428C.
Tomato item lifecycle functions use the itTomato_Logic9_* prefix, such as itTomato_Logic9_Spawned, itTomato_Logic9_Destroyed, and itTomato_Logic9_PickedUp.
Tomato motion callbacks use the itTomato_UnkMotion0_* naming pattern.
Address comments were preserved on prototypes, for example /* 2841B4 */ and /* 28428C */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use configure.py's Object(Matching, ...) marker only after the file's symbol/linkage setup supports matching.
Do not leave externally needed functions as static; static linkage changes symbol visibility and can prevent correct matching/linking behavior.
When correcting static mistakes, move prototypes to the appropriate header rather than duplicating external declarations locally.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR appears to have been merged quickly with no recorded review discussion in the excerpt.
searchable_terms: Fix incorrect static in ittomato and mark as matching
follow_up_queries: Find other item files still marked NonMatching that may contain incorrect static forward declarations.
Search for functions declared static in a .c file but also referenced from another translation unit.
Audit item headers for missing prototypes corresponding to externally visible item helper functions.
Compare nearby item files such as itheart.c, itstar.c, itbat.c, and itsword.c for matching header/linkage patterns.
